short_definition: The lifecycle contract that binds session-owned launcher descendants to the launch session that created them.
description: A Site launch session owns the runtime, projection, MCP proxy, MCP child, and helper processes it creates unless a process is explicitly host-owned or transferred. Session-owned descendants must be terminated, reaped, or explicitly transferred when the launch session ends, and future launches must not treat stale reachable descendants as fresh authority.
anti_aliases: stale reachable process as fresh surface
boundaries: This concept governs processes created by a launch session, not every host process.
Raw OS parentage is evidence but not the authority boundary by itself.
Host-owned User Site services and explicitly shared services are outside a local Site launch session unless transferred into it.

authority.notes: Defines process ownership classes, evidence stamps, cleanup semantics, preflight behavior, and stale descendant failure mode.

examples: A Sonar Site launch owns its NARS runtime server, agent-web-ui projection, and Site-scoped MCP child processes.
counterexamples: A User Site host-level MCP surface reused across naked carrier launches is host-owned, not session-owned by a Sonar launch.

confidence.basis: Observed stale Sonar MCP process demonstrated that process reachability and freshness need explicit launch ownership evidence.
